nodes.label: Lulla et al. Paired AGENTS.md Study
Rationale: Why Pull Context Beats Push for Decisions
Rationale: Four ADR Properties That Make Them Agent-Friendly
A Sablier Bug That Wasn't Sablier (blog post)
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Field-notes post tracing a sporadic 'invalid middleware' error in Sablier to a hidden startup dependency introduced by a Traefik 3.5.3 refactor.

nodes.label: Sablier
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Scale-to-zero middleware for self-hosted Docker containers; reverse-proxy wakes target containers on demand and shuts them down after an idle timeout.

nodes.label: Traefik
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Reverse-proxy hosting the Sablier plugin; v3.5.3 refactored its plugin system and introduced the startup dependency described in the post.

nodes.label: invalid middleware error
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Sporadic error 'invalid middleware type or middleware does not exist' reported by Sablier users, actually caused by Traefik plugin install failure.

nodes.label: Traefik PR #12035 (plugin system refactor)
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Merged in Traefik v3.5.3; split plugin client into Manager, Downloader, and new hash-verification plumbing, coupling plugin startup to plugins.traefik.io reachability.

nodes.label: plugins.traefik.io registry dependency
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Hidden startup dependency: Manager.InstallPlugin unconditionally calls Downloader.Download and Downloader.Check, both hitting the registry; failure aborts install.

nodes.label: Manager.InstallPlugin code block (v3.5.3)
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Affected Go code on v3.5.3 showing unconditional Download then Check calls, with failure paths returning errors that abort install.

nodes.label: sablier-traefik-repro repository
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Deterministic reproduction repo published at github.com/5queezer/sablier-traefik-repro.

nodes.label: docker-compose.netblock.yml override
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: One-line Docker override mapping plugins.traefik.io to 127.0.0.1 to force 'connection refused' and deterministically reproduce the bug.

nodes.label: Traefik issue #13005
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Fresh upstream issue filed against Traefik with deterministic reproduction, root-cause pointer to PR #12035, and a menu of fix options.

nodes.label: Traefik fix PR #13006
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Fix PR that narrows blast radius: falls back to cached archive when Download fails, keeps Check-after-successful-Download fatal, always enforces hash pinning.

nodes.label: Traefik issue #12137 (frozen-due-to-age)
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Earlier issue reporting the same root cause, auto-closed and locked by the stale-issue bot, preventing new comments.

nodes.label: ResetAll blast-radius rationale
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: One plugin's install failure calls manager.ResetAll(), which wipes the entire archives directory, disabling every plugin-backed middleware in the deployment.

nodes.label: 'sporadic = hidden dependency' heuristic
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Debugging heuristic: when timing-based reproduction fails, stop varying timing and start varying what the process can reach on the network.

nodes.label: Refile-a-locked-issue rationale
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: A locked issue is not a dead issue: filing a fresh one with repro + root-cause pointer creates a new triage signal and an open thread for future users.

nodes.label: Narrow tolerance-gate rationale
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: First draft tolerated any integrity-check failure when a cached archive existed; tightened to a 'fallback' flag set only when Download itself failed, keeping post-download Check failures fatal.

nodes.label: Coolify-on-Hetzner production deployment
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Author's box running patched Traefik plus Sablier; recovered 3.1 GiB RAM and 2 GiB swap with a ten-minute idle window on eight MCP servers and four Coolify apps.

nodes.label: Coolify reconcile loop vs Sablier conflict
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Coolify's health loop sees Sablier-stopped containers as exited:unhealthy, marks them crashed, and garbage-collects them; Sablier then 404s on wake. Compose stacks are unaffected.

nodes.label: file-provider router sync gist
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: GitHub gist with a small sync tool that regenerates the file-provider router URL to match Coolify's UUID-suffixed container names so Sablier middleware persists across redeploys.

nodes.label: Traefik docker-provider router drop gotcha
nodes.author: Christian Pojoni
nodes.summary: Docker provider drops a router the moment its backing container stops, so the Sablier middleware never fires and callers see 503; fix is a higher-priority file-provider router by docker DNS name.
